trajectory.model_output:
<python_code>
import numpy as np

# Get poses and extents for red and green cubes
red_center, _, red_extent = get_object_pose("red cube", return_bbox_extent=True)
green_center, _, green_extent = get_object_pose("green cube", return_bbox_extent=True)

# Sample grasp pose for the red cube (use its quaternion for orientation)
grasp_pos, grasp_quat = sample_grasp_pose("red cube")

# Open gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Approach the red cube with z_approach=0.1
goto_pose(grasp_pos, grasp_quat, z_approach=0.1)

# Close gripper to grasp the red cube
close_gripper()

# Lift the red cube to a safe height (at least +0.2m above the current position)
safe_z = grasp_pos[2] + 0.2
lifted_pos = np.array([grasp_pos[0], grasp_pos[1], safe_z])
goto_pose(lifted_pos, grasp_quat)

# Compute stacking height: place_z = green_center_z + half-green-height + half-red-height
place_z = green_center[2] + green_extent[2]/2 + red_extent[2]/2
placement_pos = np.array([green_center[0], green_center[1], place_z])

# Move laterally above the green cube, then approach down with z_approach=0.1
goto_pose(placement_pos, grasp_quat, z_approach=0.1)

# Release the red cube
open_gripper()

# Optionally lift the gripper after release
final_lift_z = placement_pos[2] + 0.2
final_pos = np.array([placement_pos[0], placement_pos[1], final_lift_z])
goto_pose(final_pos, grasp_quat)
</python_code>

import numpy as np

# Get poses and extents for red and green cubes
red_center, _, red_extent = get_object_pose("red cube", return_bbox_extent=True)
green_center, _, green_extent = get_object_pose("green cube", return_bbox_extent=True)

# Sample grasp pose for the red cube (use its quaternion for orientation)
grasp_pos, grasp_quat = sample_grasp_pose("red cube")

# Open gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Approach the red cube with z_approach=0.1
goto_pose(grasp_pos, grasp_quat, z_approach=0.1)

# Close gripper to grasp the red cube
close_gripper()

# Lift the red cube to a safe height (at least +0.2m above the current position)
safe_z = grasp_pos[2] + 0.2
lifted_pos = np.array([grasp_pos[0], grasp_pos[1], safe_z])
goto_pose(lifted_pos, grasp_quat)

# Compute stacking height: place_z = green_center_z + half-green-height + half-red-height
place_z = green_center[2] + green_extent[2]/2 + red_extent[2]/2
placement_pos = np.array([green_center[0], green_center[1], place_z])

# Move laterally above the green cube, then approach down with z_approach=0.1
goto_pose(placement_pos, grasp_quat, z_approach=0.1)

# Release the red cube

# Optionally lift the gripper after release

goto_pose(final_pos, grasp_quat)
